mentions.note: Data center infrastructure vendor; was trading $114 at only 8x forward earnings (cheapest 10–15% of S&P 500) four months ago. Now $465. Case study in systematic undervaluation before data center boom recognized.
Delivered blowout quarter with 32% single-session gain on data-center strength and Nvidia partnership expansion; hardware leader benefiting from AI infrastructure boom.
Michael Dell interview at NVIDIA GTC revealed exceptional AI infrastructure exposure; stock at $156 when discussed, now $305; opportunity missed due to price bias.
Data center infrastructure builder/seller. Deep-pocketed employer benefiting from AI server demand and buildout projects.
Server manufacturer—the AI factory itself. Infrastructure play critical to data center buildout.
Parabolic mover in data center/compute space; benefits from infrastructure buildout; had threatened to fly too high.
Data center hardware; Cramer incredulous at missing: 'How did I miss Dell?'
Michael Dell one of best operators; prefer over Super Micro for data center exposure; strong fundamentals.

Case study in buying quality stocks on the way down; plummeted $168→$110 on Nvidia selloff despite sound fundamentals (buybacks, vendor financing, backlog); Michael Dell's track record justifies pyramid-style accumulation on weakness.
Enterprise connectivity play to Nvidia; one of most undervalued stocks in market; bullpen stock if it retreats a bit from current levels.
Preferred alternative to Super Micro Computer for infrastructure spending; massive AI-driven pipeline ahead; Nvidia partnership driving on-prem datacenter growth.

45% EPS growth, $64B AI orders YTD, $43B backlog; Dell AI Factory model with 4,000+ customers; unique financing advantage via Dell Financial Services; innovation blitz with orchestration engine and lightning file system; outperforming Magnificent Seven except Nvidia.
Q4 beat on strong AI product sales and margin leverage (cost pass-through to customers); huge backlog; bullish 2026 guidance; +29.4% Feb, +22% single Friday rally.
Alternative to Super Micro; reports 2/26; Cramer prefers on valuation; data center AI infrastructure play without speculative overheat.
Memory chip cost spikes eating into margins; PC/server customer suffering as suppliers raise prices.
Down 30% from 3-month high; low tech PE ratio; BUT big buyer of Micron/storage with prices rising—margins getting hit; wait for earnings or storage peak before buying.

Up 155% since end of March; Michael Dell executing well; hold existing; buy further pullbacks.
